name: Homemade 8-finger Cavatelli
recipeInstructions:
1. In a large bowl, combine the ricotta and egg with salt and pepper.
2. Beat the egg and cheese together until well combined.
3. On a flat, smooth work surface (preferably wood), create a small well in the center of the flour.
4. Pour the cheese-egg mixture into the well.
5. Gradually incorporate some of the flour over the well with a fork.
6. Mix in the egg mixture gradually until it is no longer runny.
7. Use your hands to draw the sides of the mound together, working the mixture with your fingers and palms until it is smooth and well integrated.
8. Continue kneading the dough by hand until it is smooth. If the dough seems too moist, add a little more flour.
9. With clean hands, form small balls of dough about the size of a nickel (approximately 10 at a time). Cover the remaining dough with plastic wrap.
10. Place a dough ball in one palm and flatten it with the other hand.
11. Roll out the dough until it is roughly 3-inches long and about 1/4-inch thick, using opposite hands to roll.
12. Place each rolled piece of pasta on a towel.
13. Repeat the rolling process with the remaining dough.
14. Be careful not to overlap the cavatelli.
15. Allow the pasta to dry for at least 10 minutes before cooking.
16. Sprinkle with flour to prevent individual pieces from sticking.
17. Store in a cool, dry place until ready to use, up to 24 hours. To freeze leftover pasta, sprinkle liberally with flour, place in a plastic bag and seal.
